Phase E _calc_JP_PMI1 substitution deferred to §3.1.8 Stage E (would z-score and resample to Friday spine). Wired §3.1 Stage D.3 2026-04-30.

BoJ Time-Series Data Search PR01'PRCG20_2200000000 — Producer Price Index (Corporate Goods Price Index) All commodities, 2020 base. Verified live 2026-06-10 (monthly back to 1960-01; last 2026-05). Leads CPI by 2-3m; canonical JP producer-inflation gauge.

Indicator: Japan Services Producer Price Index (All Items)
Notes: BoJ Time-Series Data Search PR02'PRCS20_5200000000 — Services Producer Price Index All items basic-grouping index, 2020 base. Verified live 2026-06-10 (monthly back to 1985-01; last 2026-04). Tracks B2B service-sector inflation; complements JPN_PPI for goods-vs-services inflation read.

Indicator: BoJ Tankan: Large Enterprises / Manufacturing / Business Conditions DI (Forecast)
Units: Diffusion Index (% positive minus % negative responses)
Notes: BoJ Time-Series Data Search CO'TK99F1000601GCQ11000 — Forecast companion to JP_TANKAN1. Metadata: D.I./Business Conditions/Large Enterprises/Manufacturing/Forecast (197403 -> 202602, quarterly, TANKAN/Judgement Survey). Tankan forecast DI = respondent's outlook for next quarter — leads the Actual companion by one quarter. Pinned from CO-db getMetadata on 2026-06-11.

Indicator: BoJ Tankan: Large Enterprises / Non-Manufacturing / Business Conditions DI (Actual result)
Units: Diffusion Index (% positive minus % negative responses)
Notes: BoJ Time-Series Data Search CO'TK99F2000601GCQ01000 — Non-manufacturing counterpart to JP_TANKAN1. Metadata: D.I./Business Conditions/Large Enterprises/Nonmanufacturing/Actual result (198302 -> 202601, quarterly, TANKAN/Judgement Survey). Captures services/retail/construction-side cycle — complements the manufacturing DI for a goods-vs-services regime read. Pinned from CO-db getMetadata on 2026-06-11.

Indicator: BoJ Tankan: Large Enterprises / Non-Manufacturing / Business Conditions DI (Forecast)
Units: Diffusion Index (% positive minus % negative responses)
Notes: BoJ Time-Series Data Search CO'TK99F2000601GCQ11000 — Forecast companion to JP_TANKAN_LNFG. Metadata: D.I./Business Conditions/Large Enterprises/Nonmanufacturing/Forecast (198303 -> 202602, quarterly, TANKAN/Judgement Survey). Forecast leads Actual by one quarter; useful for nowcasting services-side cycle turns. Pinned from CO-db getMetadata on 2026-06-11.

Indicator: BoJ Tankan: Small Enterprises / Manufacturing / Business Conditions DI (Actual result)
Units: Diffusion Index (% positive minus % negative responses)
Notes: BoJ Time-Series Data Search CO'TK99F1000601GCQ03000 — Small-enterprise counterpart to JP_TANKAN1. Metadata: D.I./Business Conditions/Small Enterprises/Manufacturing/Actual result (196703 -> 202601, quarterly, TANKAN/Judgement Survey). Smaller manufacturers respond faster to domestic demand swings than large exporters; Large-minus-Small spread is a classic Japan-domestic vs export-cycle signal. Pinned from CO-db getMetadata on 2026-06-11.

Indicator: BoJ Tankan: Small Enterprises / Non-Manufacturing / Business Conditions DI (Actual result)
Units: Diffusion Index (% positive minus % negative responses)
Notes: BoJ Time-Series Data Search CO'TK99F2000601GCQ03000 — Completes the 4-quadrant Tankan view (Large/Small x Mfg/Non-mfg). Metadata: D.I./Business Conditions/Small Enterprises/Nonmanufacturing/Actual result (198302 -> 202601, quarterly, TANKAN/Judgement Survey). Small-NFG is the cleanest read on household-facing/SME-services sentiment. Pinned from CO-db getMetadata on 2026-06-11.

Indicator: Japan Machinery Orders (Cabinet Office Orders Received for Machinery — private sector ex volatile)
Notes: §3.1.3 Growth axis extension. 2026-06-15 fix: the previously-registered statsDataId 0003355224 was the ANNUAL fiscal-year Original-Series table (no monthly / no SA) — getMetaInfo: cat02=系列 offered only OriginalSeries and time=Fiscal year, so it resolved a stale annual slice. Re-pointed to the monthly SA sibling 0003355222 = 長期系列 主要需要者別機械受注額(原系列・季調系列、月次), Cabinet Office Machinery Orders (stat 00100401). Slice cdCat01=160 (需要者分類 民間需要(船舶・電力を除く) = private-sector excl. ships & electric power) + cdCat02=100 (系列 季調系列 = seasonally adjusted). Verified 2026-06-15: monthly (median_gap≈31); last obs 2026-03 vs the old annual slice stuck 2025-10.

Catalogue URL https://www.e-stat.go.jp/stat-search/database?stat_infid=000031390838&layout=dataset&statdisp_id=0003138782. Notes: §3.1.3 Growth axis extension 2026-06-10. Indicator: France MFI rate — new loans to households for house purchase
Notes: PROVISIONAL — Banque de France migrated from the legacy IBM API Connect stack (api.webstat.banque-france.fr/webstat-fr/v1) to an Opendatasoft Explore v2.1 instance at https://webstat.banque-france.fr/api/explore/v2.1/. The public catalogue exposes only one dataset (tableaux_rapports_preetablis = archived report attachments); the 37000+ time series including MFI lending rates are gated behind the developer-portal login. Once BDF_API_KEY is in the runtime: query the catalogue to locate the MFI interest-rate dataset_id (likely something like webstat-bdf-mir or mfi-interest-rates-france) browse its schema to find the SDMX dimension columns then replace this series_id with the form '<dataset_id>|<odsql_where>' encoding the original SDMX dot-key M.FR.B.A2C.A.R.A.2250.EUR.N (households for house purchase). Until verified the fetcher logs a PROVISIONAL warning and skips this row.

Shiller's headline CAPE (P/E10) ratio computed from S&P 500 real price ÷ 10-year average real earnings. Sourced from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook (http://www.econ.yale.edu/~shiller/data/ie_data.xls). Long-history monthly back to 1881. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10 — parser verified offline against the documented sheet layout; live cross-validation pending the first credentialed CI run (sandbox blocks Yale + the shillerdata.com / datahub.io / posix4e mirrors).

Monthly S&P Composite price level from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook. Long-history common-frame counterpart to daily SPX tickers; back to Jan 1871. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10 — cross-validation pending live CI run.
Monthly S&P Composite trailing dividend from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook. Back to Jan 1871. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10 — cross-validation pending live CI run.

Monthly S&P Composite trailing earnings from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook. Back to Jan 1871. Used as the denominator anchor for the CAPE definition. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10 — cross-validation pending live CI run.

Long-run US CPI from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook (BLS CPI-U linked to Warren-Pearson back to 1871). Pre-1913 portion is not BLS-native but Shiller's standard splice; intended as a pre-WW2 cross-validation anchor for regime-AA Phase 0c, NOT a replacement for the modern BLS CUSR0000SA0 column (which remains the canonical USA_CPI_INDEX). Wired §3.13 2026-06-10.

Long-run US 10-year Treasury yield from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook (FRED GS10 spliced to NBER long-rate series pre-1953). Pre-1953 portion is not FRED-native; intended as a pre-WW2 cross-validation anchor for regime-AA Phase 0c. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10.

Kenneth French data library — US market excess return back to 1926-07 from the F-F 3-Factor monthly file. ZIP: https://mba.tuck.dartmouth.edu/pages/faculty/ken.french/ftp/F-F_Research_Data_Factors_CSV.zip. Wired §3.11 2026-06-10 — driven by regime-AA Phase 0 long-run factor requirement. Switched 2026-06-11 from the 5-Factors_2x3 file (which only starts 1963-07) to the 3-Factor file to honour the 1926-07 history brief. RMW/CMA stay on the 5-factor ZIP (they only exist from 1963-07).

Small-minus-Big size factor from the F-F 3-Factor monthly file (ZIP F-F_Research_Data_Factors_CSV.zip). History from 1926-07. Note: the 3-Factor SMB is computed on the 2x3 size/book-to-market sort and differs slightly from the 5-Factor SMB which averages three 2x3 sorts (size/BM + size/OP + size/Inv) — we use the 3-Factor version to preserve the 1926-07 history.
High-minus-Low book/market value factor from the F-F 3-Factor monthly file (ZIP F-F_Research_Data_Factors_CSV.zip). History from 1926-07.

Robust-minus-Weak operating-profitability factor from the F-F 5-Factor 2x3 monthly file (ZIP F-F_Research_Data_5_Factors_2x3_CSV.zip). History from 1963-07 (RMW post-dates Mkt/SMB/HML — operating-profitability data unavailable earlier).
Conservative-minus-Aggressive investment factor from the F-F 5-Factor 2x3 monthly file (ZIP F-F_Research_Data_5_Factors_2x3_CSV.zip). History from 1963-07.
One-month Treasury-bill return packaged with the F-F 3-Factor monthly file (ZIP F-F_Research_Data_Factors_CSV.zip). History from 1926-07.

JST Macrohistory R6 column `gdp` — annual nominal GDP. Codebook §3. The JST `gdp` variable is NOMINAL GDP (current prices, local currency), not real — relabelled 2026-06-17 per label-vs-data audit finding #4 (empirically USA grew ~64x 1950->2015, consistent with nominal not real ~6x). No live calculator consumes these columns (historical cross-validation anchor). For a real-GDP anchor, JST R6 also publishes `rgdpmad` (Maddison real GDP).

The JST `gdp` variable is NOMINAL GDP (current prices, local currency), not real — relabelled 2026-06-17 per label-vs-data audit finding #4 (empirically USA grew ~64x 1950->2015, consistent with nominal not real ~6x). No live calculator consumes these columns (historical cross-validation anchor). For a real-GDP anchor, JST R6 also publishes `rgdpmad` (Maddison real GDP).

Indicator: Atlanta Fed GDPNow — US Real GDP Growth (Q/Q SAAR)
Notes: Atlanta Fed GDPNow real-time nowcast of US Q/Q annualised real GDP growth. Source: GDPTrackingModelDataAndForecasts.xlsx on the GDPNow homepage (https://www.atlantafed.org/cqer/research/gdpnow — canonical xlsx path verified 2026-06-11 by scraping the page's Downloads section). Updated 5-10 times per quarter on weekdays following key data releases (ISM, FT900, Retail Trade, Wholesale Trade, Construction Spending, Housing Starts, Durable Goods, Personal Income & Outlays, IP, Existing-Home Sales). The raw cadence is irregular within-quarter and silent between quarters; we declare frequency=Weekly because the compute_macro_market calculator resamples to weekly Friday (matching the rest of the macro_market spine) and forward-fills between updates. freshness_override_days=14 because the model is silent between the final-nowcast-of-Q and initial-nowcast-of-Q+1 days (typically a ~3-5 business-day window around BEA's advance estimate); 14 days catches a stale endpoint without false-positives during the normal quiet windows. Wired §3.1.4 2026-06-11 — first §3.1.4 nowcast via a NEW primary-source fetcher.

Indicator: New York Fed Staff Nowcast — US Real GDP Growth (Q/Q SAAR)
Notes: New York Fed Staff Nowcast real-time nowcast of US Q/Q annualised real GDP growth. Source: New-York-Fed-Staff-Nowcast_download_data.xlsx on the Staff Nowcast homepage (https://www.newyorkfed.org/research/policy/nowcast — canonical xlsx path verified 2026-06-11 by SERP-resolving the page's Downloads section). Published weekly on Fridays during tracking quarters; silent for ~3-5 business days around BEA's advance estimate between the final-nowcast-of-Q and initial-nowcast-of-Q+1 windows. The raw cadence is weekly with quiet windows; we declare frequency=Weekly because the compute_macro_market calculator resamples to weekly Friday (matching the rest of the macro_market spine) and forward-fills between updates. freshness_override_days=14 mirrors US_GDPNOW — same nowcast quiet-window pattern. Second §3.1.4 nowcast fetcher; provides the US_NOWCAST1 second-opinion read on US growth alongside US_GDPNOW1 (Atlanta Fed GDPNow). Wired §3.1.4 2026-06-11.
